content: Diagnosis primarily clinical using BMI calculated as weight(kg)/height(m)². WHO classification: underweight <18.5, normal 18.5-24.9, overweight 25-29.9, obesity class I 30-34.9, class II 35-39.9, class III ≥40 kg/m². Asian populations have lower cutoffs (overweight ≥23, obesity ≥25) due to higher metabolic risk at lower BMI. Waist circumference adds risk stratification: elevated ≥102cm men/≥88cm women (lower for Asian: ≥90cm men/≥80cm women). Body composition assessment when BMI misleading (athletes, elderly): DEXA gold standard for body fat percentage (obesity defined as >25% men, >35% women). Evaluation should assess obesity-related comorbidities: fasting glucose/HbA1c, lipid panel, liver enzymes (NAFLD), blood pressure, sleep apnea screening, joint symptoms, depression screening. Identify secondary causes if atypical presentation: hypothyroidism, Cushing syndrome, medications (antipsychotics, antidepressants, steroids).
